title: Chrysanthemum bornmuelleri
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum bornmuelleri is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of China. The classification of Chrysanthemum bornmuelleri is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, class Magnoliopsida, order Asterales, family Asteraceae, genus Chrysanthemum, and species C. bornmuelleri. The classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships and characteristics. By grouping similar species together, botanists can better comprehend the plant's diversity and adaptability.

intro: Chrysanthemum bornmuelleri, also known as Bornmüller's chrysanthemum, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects.
